source: independent (non-author) pred-scout-coach-critic reviewing data/postgame/*.json coaching against the match facts; preference-based / ungrounded lines flagged; grounded rewrites applied back

0d4036b5-26c7-47ea-ab47-e6be8a9c4985.quote: Eight fights won and six of them cashed into a prize inside 90 seconds — GRIM.exe's 17/1 became Fangtooth, Mini Prime, Orb Prime and a 27-minute close.

0d4036b5-26c7-47ea-ab47-e6be8a9c4985.quote: This is the blueprint for converting: you won eight of eleven clustered fights, lost just one, and six of the eight wins bought a major within 90 seconds — the 13.7 fight at their outer tower, the 16.3 Mini Prime sweep (4–0 even with your fighters at 2 items online to their 4), your Fangtooth at 24.4 and their inner tower at 26.6, stacking Fangtooth at 14, 19 and 26, Mini Prime at 17, Orb Prime at 21 and two Genesis cores. They won one fight all game and cashed nothing. The one sharpen: every early death was a solo catch outside any fight — Phase at 3.9 (first blood), Steel at 4.5 and 6.6, Gadget at 17.7 — rotate in pairs and that team gets zero kills before the fights even start.

309dc19a-9130-4f28-863a-29fef5cdd55a.quote: Countess had the lane all game and Rampage made it worse — both of your solo catches (first blood at 3.9 and again at 14.4) were his, and the 9.8 death at their Fangtooth helped buy the 10-minute take. 3b299c1c-84af-42e2-9bc5-962582666f7b.quote: The draft was close to even: jungle favored (Grux 51.5% into Yin), support a touch unfavored (Zinx 50.4% into Riktor), and the lanes only a hair behind — Murdock 48.4% into Sparrow, TwinBlast 48.5% into Fey, Bayle 47.6% into Steel. You won the 16.5 Fangtooth fight (2-0) and kept the majors close (24-29 against), but still lost structures 6 to 14 — buffs that never became towers. 561dab0b-1487-48ec-9db9-314309199f14.quote: Same squad, opposite result, and the macro shows why: when fights went down you took the major in the same window. You lost the carry lane (Sparrow theirs all game vs Murdock) and won anyway — The Fey mid (5/3/10, team-high 27.8k) and Iggy offlane (7/4/8, 28k) carried the tempo, Khaimera anchored the objective damage, and Steel's 12 assists glued it. One thing to keep tightening: at 10m, 13m, 24m the macro still flagged The Fey alive and ahead but not yet at the fight — when he rotated, you snowballed. Keep grouping on the prize and this is repeatable.

d93c02c4-beb0-4533-9bdc-cd776a1efe4b.quote: The fighting was never the problem: eight clustered fights, eight wins, zero losses — the enemy's entire game was solo catches (seven of you caught rotating alone; they never won a fight). The story is the cash-in: five of your wins (19.7, 24.9, 34.5, 41.2, 41.8) turned into nothing within 90 seconds, and at 41.2 you were still winning fights at your own inhibitor and core. The wins you did convert — Fangtooth at 24 after the 23.0 fight, Fangtooth at 32 after the 31.0 defense, Primal Fangtooth at 40 after 39.1 — are what finally broke them. Repeat the fight discipline; the one sharpen is treating the 90 seconds after a won fight as tower time, and cutting the solo picks — the catches at 27, 27.8 and 29.9 (three of you, alone, in under three minutes) are exactly the stall that kept a beaten team alive.

The story is the cash-in: only three of the eight wins bought a prize within 90 seconds, and at 41.2 and 41.8 you were still winning fights at your own inhibitor and core. Repeat the fight discipline; the one sharpen is treating the 90 seconds after a won fight as tower time, and cutting the solo picks — the catches at 27, 27.8 and 29.9 (three of you, alone, in under three minutes) are exactly the stall that kept a beaten team alive.
